message:
fcp-mesh/admission: inline u64::min in const fn for current nightly

`PeerUsage::prev_window_weight` is `const fn` and was calling
`elapsed.min(WINDOW_MS)`, which dispatches to `Ord::min`. `Ord` is
not yet stable as a const trait on the current nightly
(rust-lang #143874), so the call breaks compilation:

  error[E0658]: cannot call non-const fn `<u64 as std::cmp::Ord>::min`
                in constant functions
  error: `Ord` is not yet stable as a const trait

This blocked the entire `cargo test -p fcp-mesh` lib-test build.
Inline the comparison as a plain `if/else` so the function and its
const callers (`effective_bytes_in_window`, etc.) keep compiling
without needing `#![feature(const_cmp)]`.

Closes flywheel_connectors-bk3m6.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
